name: The Great Gluten Turkey, Part One
recipeIngredient: Approx. 14 cups water or 8 cups vital wheat gluten flour

Yeast flakes (1 cup), Salt (1 tbsp), Ginger (1/2 tsp), Turmeric (1/2 tsp), Black pepper (1 t), Marjoram (1 t), Tarragon (1 t), Paprika (1 t), Rosemary (1 t), Sage (2 tsp), Celery seed (2 tsp), Garlic powder (2 tsp), Onion powder (2 tsp)
Sage (1 1/2 tsp), Marjoram (1 1/2 tsp), Thyme (1 1/2 tsp), Salt (1 t)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak and reconstitute the yuba to a soft, pliable texture.
2. Make gluten using either wheat flour or vital wheat gluten flour.
3. Roll the gluten into a rectangle approximately 15 inches long and 8 to 10 inches wide.
4. Sprinkle with 1/3 cup yeast flavoring (unless added in with vital wheat gluten).
5. Roll up lengthwise.
6. Place the roll in a large pot, pour over it the gallon of water and remaining 2/3 cup yeast flavoring.
7. Cover, bring to a boil, turn down heat, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours (up to two days before serving).
8. Remove gluten roll from stock, saving for gravy.
9. Untie cheesecloth, unroll "turkey," fill with stuffing or mound on greased baking sheet.
10. Place stuffed gluten on greased baking sheet.
11. Reconstitute yuba by soaking for 5 minutes in water, then brush the "turkey" with melted margarine.
12. Baste while baking using the remaining margarine-wine mixture (optional yeast flavoring).
